metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1686
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This paper shows data on the TUBB2B-brdp/+ heterozygous mouse which has a N247S point-mutation. This is not a full loss-of-function mutation (see additional ref) but the homozygous mutant shows many brain abnormalities that are in line with a loss of tubulin function. In this paper, Fig5 the heterozygous mouse shows impaired LTP in the CA1 hippocampus synapses by field recordings. This is mirrored by an impaired performance in the morris water maze task (fig 2), which tests hippocampal spatial learning. The distribution of cells in the CA1 area is also affected (Fig6).

24/10/2017 Pim
- The authors show no data that sheds light onto the mechanism by which TUBB2B has this (most likely) regulatory role on ST. One might speculate that this is due to aberrant axonal or dendritic transport, but it might also be developmental differences. I agree to the quite vague term 'modulation of chemical synaptic transmission (GO:0050804)' to represent the BP of this tubulin protein.. Experimental description: M&M: "Adult male mice with heterozygous missense mutations in the beta tubulin, 2b gene (Tubb2b-brdp/+) and wild-type (WT) littermates were used as subjects."
